results.title: Orchestria: AI music engine with granular stem control | Product Hunt
results.content:
Our Approach & Evolution  
Orchestria flips this paradigm on its head. It generates music as individual, sample-accurate audio stems (Drums, Bass, and Melody) — all in studio-grade, 24-bit / 44.1kHz quality.  
While developing our system, we found manual dials to be rather cumbersome, which is why we invented the "Agentic Flip". Thanks to our AI agents, you can give commands to individual stems using natural language prompts (e.g., "Pluckier bassline", or "Change the melody lead instrument into retro synth"). Our AI will make the exact changes to the MIDI and VST instruments automatically, while keeping your overall track untouched. [...] We'd Love Your Feedback On:  
1. The Agentic Flip: How does the natural language stem-control workflow feel to you?  
2. Deep Obsidian Player: What do you think about the interactive layout and visualization?  
3. Future Tech: Which VST / synthesizer integrations do you want to see us deploy next?  
  
We will be hanging out in the comments all day. Let's make some music together! 🎧🎹  
  
Hundreds of people were already on our waitlist. Join us!  

Thami Benjelloun
Mailwarm

Mailwarm

Is there any content fingerprinting or proof of ownership you provide if someone ever disputes a track?

Batu Akdoğan
Orchestria

Orchestria

@thamibenjelloun Yes, absolutely. If you ever face a copyright dispute or need to prove ownership, we provide: [...] When we looked at the current generative music space (Suno, Udio, etc.), we realized they all hit the exact same brick wall: they treat music like pixels, rendering a single, uneditable stereo file.   
We knew that for real studio production, this is a dead-end. Creators don't want a final master; they want modular components.

To fix this, we avoided standard prompt-to-audio wrappers entirely. Instead, we built an agentic audio pipeline that spins up real, professional-grade, headless VST instruments (like Vital and Surge XT) directly inside our production servers.

4

16

Orchestria
Batu Akdoğan

25d ago

results.title: Sitefire: Marketing suite for the agentic web - Product Hunt
results.content:
Vincent Jeltsch
Sitefire

Sitefire

@jscanzi Our customers currently do a final review of the draft. We don't auto-publish quite yet. But we can check what they changed before publishing each time. And we our agent can reflect on that to refine the context over time.

On your user review angle: do you know any good studies on this? Would be super cool to look at some data!

Devon Kelley [...] Generally how it works: we run hundreds of "probing prompts" every day to collect answers. These probing prompts aim to be realistic questions your customers would ask. And then we extract all relevant data.

Additionally we are working on network-log integrations which will be super interesting to add to the mix!

Taimur Haider

@jochenmadler  @vincent\_jeltsch1 Got it. The network-log integration will be really powerful.

I’ve been thinking about how some of these insights could impact homepage visibility.

I’m sharing a few of these observations on LinkedIn.   
  
Would like to hear your take when you have a moment.

Denis Akindinov [...] Sitefire

results.title: MuleRun Competitors & Alternatives (2026) - Product Hunt
results.content:
For content and strategy work, Claude’s tone control and long-form coherence can be a decisive advantage over more automation-centric products. Choose it when the primary bottleneck is judgment and synthesis—turning messy inputs into clear plans, polished copy, or working code—rather than connecting dozens of SaaS tools together.

### Best for

Best for founders, engineers, and content teams who prioritize deep reasoning, coding help, and long-form writing.

### Standout features

 ✓Strong long-context reasoning and synthesis
 ✓Claude Code for agentic development workflows
 ✓High-quality long-form writing and editing
 ✓Style and voice consistency for content
 ✓Tool connector ecosystem via MCP

## Trace

TraceTrace

Workflow Automations for the Human 👾 AI Workforce

4.7 · 38 reviews [...] Make also brings mature operational controls like scheduling and the ability to test parts of a scenario before deploying changes. If reliability comes from precise workflows, not agent autonomy, Make provides a proven “automation operating system” style experience that can outperform more agentic setups for repeatable business processes.

### Best for

Ideal for ops and growth teams running complex, high-volume multi-app automations that need deterministic control.

### Standout features

 ✓Visual scenario builder for complex workflows
 ✓Large integration and module ecosystem
 ✓Built-in scheduling and run controls
 ✓Test and iterate sections of scenarios
 ✓Extensibility for custom integrations

## Claude by Anthropic

Claude by AnthropicClaude by Anthropic [...] Claude by AnthropicClaude by Anthropic

A family of foundational AI models

5.0 · 864 reviews

Learn more →

Claude is the alternative when the “engine” you need is top-tier reasoning and writing quality, not a workflow canvas or a persistent VM agent. For many teams, the fastest path past MuleRun-style orchestration is letting a model handle the hard cognitive work—planning, drafting, refactoring, and problem-solving—while existing tools handle execution.

For developers, Claude Code adds an agentic layer that can move work forward in a structured, step-by-step way, making it valuable for building features, fixing bugs, and accelerating implementation. It’s especially strong when projects require long context, consistent architectural decisions, and high-quality code output.

results.title: Heizen Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Fundraisly

#### What's great

AKANKSHA YADAV

#### What's great

This is a really strong “AI technical co-founder” concept. I like how Heizen covers the complete software lifecycle—from product planning and UI design to code generation, QA, deployment, and even security/compliance audits. That multi-agent approach feels genuinely agentic instead of just being a coding assistant. Would be great to know how well it handles real-world repo updates, bug fixes, and long-term maintenance workflows.

Sudhanshu Kumar

#### What's great [...] I’m a final‑year engineering student working on Python and ML projects, so the idea of an “AI technical co‑founder” made up of multiple agents is really interesting. What stands out in Heizen is how the PM, design, coding, QA, and DevOps agents work together to turn messy discussions into roadmaps, UI mockups, code, tests, and deployments, instead of treating each step as a separate tool. That feels much closer to how real product teams operate, especially for early‑stage founders who are juggling all these roles themselves. I also like that reviewers call out the PM Agent for capturing meetings and structuring work, because in my own projects that’s usually the part that gets ignored while everyone obsesses over code. I’m curious how well Heizen plugs into an existing codebase and stack, [...] how well Heizen plugs into an existing codebase and stack, and where you currently draw the line between what the agents handle autonomously and what still needs a human engineer for complex edge cases and custom integrations.
